title: Wordbank
text:
Citation
Frank, M. C., Braginsky, M., Yurovsky, D., & Marchman, V. A. (2017). Wordbank: An open repository for developmental vocabulary data. Journal of Child Language, 44(3), 677–694.

title: About
text:
Wordbank is an open database of children’s vocabulary growth, built on data from the MacArthur-Bates Communicative Development Inventories (CDIs) and their adaptations across many languages. It is developed and maintained by the Language and Cognition Lab at Stanford University.
This site is a static rebuild of wordbank.stanford.edu on the datapages infrastructure: data are hosted on Redivis and all visualization runs in your browser. Source at langcog/wordbank-datapage.

title: About
text:
Citation
Frank, M. C., Braginsky, M., Yurovsky, D., & Marchman, V. A. (2017). Wordbank: An open repository for developmental vocabulary data. Journal of Child Language, 44(3), 677–694.
See also: Frank, M. C., Braginsky, M., Yurovsky, D., & Marchman, V. A. (2021). Variability and Consistency in Early Language Learning: The Wordbank Project. MIT Press.

title: Item Trajectories
Data
text:
All Wordbank data are hosted on the datapages.wordbank Redivis dataset, versioned and freely downloadable. Tables can be filtered and exported directly on Redivis; for programmatic access see the wordbankr R package or the Redivis API.
Most datasets are shared under a CC-BY license; some carry CC-BY-NC (see the license column of the datasets table).
